jokes.text: I thought global warming would take care of it. Al Gore can't do anything right.
jokes.setup: Family is freezing because heating bill wasn't paid
jokes.punchline: Homer blames Al Gore for global warming not being effective enough
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - absurd reasoning with confident delivery. Character-specific stupidity that feels earned.

jokes.text: Big & Tall Halloween Costume catalogue
jokes.setup: Homer listing magazines to burn for heat
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific catalogue reveals Homer's shopping habits
jokes.explanation: Solid character detail joke - economical and specific to Homer's size and interests.

jokes.text: Like which kid's their favorite! It's Lisa.
jokes.setup: Parents are allowed to keep some secrets
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately reveals their favorite child
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff with Homer's inability to keep secrets. Dark family humor delivered efficiently.

jokes.text: Yeah, what happened while I was chillin' in Dad's junk?
jokes.setup: Family discussing timeline inconsistencies about Bart's birth
jokes.punchline: Bart's crude reference to being an unborn sperm
jokes.explanation: Bart's characteristic inappropriate humor. Crude but character-appropriate and unexpectedly philosophical.

jokes.text: Come on. More turbulent than now? We're in every kind of therapy.
jokes.setup: Parents describing their past as 'turbulent'
jokes.punchline: Bart points out their current dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the Simpson family's ongoing dysfunction. Self-aware without being too meta.

jokes.text: The Iraq war was over once and for all, a struggling Matt Groening created Futurama
jokes.setup: Homer describing the wonderful 1990s
jokes.punchline: Reference to show creator and Iraq War's false ending
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about the show's creator and political commentary. Clever but breaks character slightly.

jokes.text: I'll know who you were with in less than six hours! Unless someone picks up the phone.
jokes.setup: 90s flashback showing technology
jokes.punchline: Dial-up internet frustration perfectly captured
jokes.explanation: Perfect 90s technology joke that anyone who lived through dial-up will appreciate. Well-structured nostalgia humor.

jokes.text: With you, baby, Top Ramen tastes like Cup O' Noodles.
jokes.setup: Young Homer trying to be romantic about their poor food situation
jokes.punchline: Comparing two essentially identical cheap noodle brands
jokes.explanation: Homer's romantic cluelessness perfectly captured. Economical joke about brand differences that don't exist.

jokes.text: I want to wait until I'm married, or at least really drunk.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining she's not ready for physical intimacy
jokes.punchline: Drunk as acceptable alternative to marriage
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of moral stance with practical alternative. Character-appropriate humor for young Marge.

jokes.text: Sonic The hedgehog says 'Wait until marriage'.
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting they follow celebrity role models
jokes.punchline: Video game character as moral authority
jokes.explanation: Absurd 90s reference with Homer's characteristic misunderstanding of appropriate role models.

jokes.text: inoffensive urban light hip-hop smooth grooves
jokes.setup: Homer describing his musical aspirations
jokes.punchline: Overly verbose description of bland 90s R&B
jokes.explanation: Satirizes 90s music marketing speak and Homer's pretensions. Accurate cultural parody.

jokes.text: I'll make rub to you / Backrub all night long!
jokes.setup: The band performing their smooth R&B song
jokes.punchline: Innocent backrub lyrics that sound sexual
jokes.explanation: Double entendre humor typical of 90s R&B parody. Appropriately cheesy and cringe-worthy.

jokes.text: I thought you were telling me you wanted to apply yourself to making a collage.
jokes.setup: Marge saying she told Homer about applying to college
jokes.punchline: Homer's misunderstanding of 'college' as 'collage'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer misunderstanding - shows his intellectual limitations while being believable miscommunication.

jokes.text: And as I recall, I was against it.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his opposition to Marge's 'collage' making
jokes.punchline: His continued commitment to the misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that shows Homer's stubbornness and commitment to his misunderstanding.

jokes.text: You're dead! Die! Die! Die! Suck my beam!
jokes.setup: Homer working for his father's laser tag business
jokes.punchline: Abe violently attacking Homer with laser tag gun
jokes.explanation: Classic Abe Simpson cruelty toward Homer. Character-appropriate workplace abuse with laser tag twist.

jokes.text: This counts as your break.
jokes.setup: Abe finishing his violent assault on Homer
jokes.punchline: Claiming the beating counts as Homer's work break
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny workplace exploitation joke. Economical and perfectly captures Abe's cruelty.

jokes.text: And that is why The Lord of the Rings can never be filmed.
jokes.setup: College intellectual discussion atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Professor's confident prediction that proved completely wrong
jokes.explanation: Brilliant hindsight joke - audience knows LOTR films were massively successful. Great historical irony.

jokes.text: Good. Now throw them all away!
jokes.setup: Professor asking if students bought required textbooks
jokes.punchline: Immediately telling them to discard the expensive books
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with excellent economy. Captures pretentious professor behavior and student frustration.

jokes.text: Wrong! They were all white, Protestant, property-owning males.
jokes.setup: Students giving correct historical answers about Founding Fathers
jokes.punchline: Professor's reductive focus on demographics rather than ideas
jokes.explanation: Satirizes academic reductionism - dismissing substantive answers to focus solely on identity categories.

jokes.text: Evil
jokes.setup: Professor explaining Founding Fathers were white Protestant males
jokes.punchline: Student's simple, dramatic reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction beat. Captures campus political correctness with economic delivery.

jokes.text: Fall semester, you just got interesting.
jokes.setup: Professor's radical interpretation of American history
jokes.punchline: Marge's excited reaction treating it like entertainment
jokes.explanation: Shows Marge's earnest enthusiasm for learning, even when it's questionable teaching.

jokes.text: Even Walt Disney?
jokes.setup: Marge explains every US president has been a straight white man
jokes.punchline: Homer's confusion about Walt Disney being president
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer confusion - mishearing 'president' facts and assuming Disney was president. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Really? I thought it was written by losers.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining history is written by the winners
jokes.punchline: Homer's innocent confusion about who writes history
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer misunderstanding that reveals his skewed perspective on success and education.

jokes.text: If my beads are parted, come right in.
jokes.setup: Professor giving Marge his office hours
jokes.punchline: Using hippie bead curtain as door signal
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that establishes professor's hippie persona. Visual gag that works in context.

jokes.text: I never heard that noise before. Are you sick?
jokes.setup: Homer presumably making jealous/angry noise about Professor August
jokes.punchline: Marge's innocent misinterpretation of Homer's jealous growling
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy beat inferred from dialogue. Shows Marge's obliviousness to Homer's jealousy.

jokes.text: Just shut up, monkey man, and make the gas flow.
jokes.setup: Gas worker offering to tell romantic story while working
jokes.punchline: His bitter summary of how people usually respond
jokes.explanation: Good minor character moment. Captures working-class resentment and social disconnection effectively.

jokes.text: Could a tribe of bushmen have invented penicillin? Maybe they already have. But no one asked them.
jokes.setup: Discussion about marginalized Third World contributions
jokes.punchline: Professor's pseudo-intellectual non-answer that sounds profound
jokes.explanation: Good satire of academic pretentiousness. Captures how professors deflect difficult questions with meaningless profundity.

jokes.text: no soup for you! Because I am 'master of my domain'. Newman.
jokes.setup: Homer defending situation comedy against professor's criticism
jokes.punchline: String of Seinfeld references as counterargument
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about sitcoms within a sitcom. References feel somewhat forced but capture 90s Seinfeld obsession.

jokes.text: Can't I get all Zima'd up and have my best girl put salve on my laser burns?
jokes.setup: Homer wanting to relax after work
jokes.punchline: Perfect 90s reference combining Zima and laser tag injuries
jokes.explanation: Excellent period-specific humor. 'Zima'd up' is perfect 90s slang and laser burns callback works well.

jokes.text: Are the lasers supposed to burn you? Apparently if all the kids fire at your stomach at once, it adds up.
jokes.setup: Marge's innocent question about laser tag safety
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact explanation of workplace abuse
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff structure with Homer's casual acceptance of workplace danger. Darkly funny.

jokes.text: Actually, Marge, if I may deconstruct that letter. When he writes 'I miss you,' 'I' is the subject and 'you' is the object. You are his object.
jokes.setup: Professor analyzing Homer's sweet note to Marge
jokes.punchline: Turning basic grammar into pseudo-feminist critique
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of academic overanalysis. Perfect example of how professors can make anything sound oppressive.

jokes.text: It's 'Juan' Miro! Stupid townie!
jokes.setup: Homer mispronouncing artist's name as 'Joan' instead of 'Juan'
jokes.punchline: College student's pretentious correction and insult
jokes.explanation: Good town vs. gown class conflict humor. Captures academic snobbery and cultural gatekeeping.

jokes.text: If you did the former better we'd use the latter less.
jokes.setup: Homer pointing out townies cook food and clean bathrooms
jokes.punchline: Student's pretentious comeback about food quality and bathroom usage
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows student's intellectual arrogance. Well-structured insult with logical flow.

jokes.text: This place is nothing like Animal House! I smashed a beatnik's guitar and he sued me for damages! Successfully!
jokes.setup: Homer complaining college isn't like the movie Animal House
jokes.punchline: His destructive behavior backfired legally
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Homer's expectation vs reality. The 'Successfully!' tag makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Guitar Rock Utilizing Nihilist Grunge Energies. Or, as I call it: Grunge!
jokes.setup: Homer describing his new music direction with pretentious name
jokes.punchline: The acronym spells out the already-existing genre name
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that satirizes both pretentious band names and Homer's belief he invented grunge. Well-crafted acronym joke.

jokes.text: Kurt? Kurt? It's Marvin! Your cousin, Marvin Cobain! You know that new sound you're looking for? Well, listen to this!
jokes.setup: Reference to Back to the Future's Marvin Berry calling Chuck Berry
jokes.punchline: Same gag but with Kurt Cobain stealing Homer's grunge music
jokes.explanation: Clever parody of iconic Back to the Future scene. Good structure but feels somewhat derivative.

jokes.text: Hey. Will you sign my boobs? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Homer has become a grunge rock star
jokes.punchline: Casual request and acceptance of inappropriate autograph location
jokes.explanation: Standard rock star groupie joke. Economical but not particularly original or character-specific.

jokes.text: I'll take the Enron stock; you take the Microsoft stock.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge dividing possessions during breakup
jokes.punchline: Homer choosing the stock that would become worthless
jokes.explanation: Excellent hindsight joke about 90s financial decisions. Homer's bad luck/judgment perfectly captured.

jokes.text: I just want a tentacle.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge deciding who gets their pet Cutie-puss
jokes.punchline: Homer's modest request for just one tentacle of their octopus
jokes.explanation: Dark divorce humor with Homer's pathetic request. Economical and emotionally effective.

jokes.text: I don't sell booze no more. This is a cigar bar now. Fine. Cohiba me.
jokes.setup: Homer going to Moe's for a drink during depression
jokes.punchline: 90s trend of cigar bars and Homer's adaptation
jokes.explanation: Good 90s period humor. 'Cohiba me' is a clever verbal adaptation of Homer's drinking habit.

jokes.text: I wasn't trying to steal your watch. I-I was just comin' on to ya.
jokes.setup: Barney appearing to steal Homer's watch
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was actually a romantic advance
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Barney's desperate loneliness. Character-appropriate and well-structured.

jokes.text: I want your knowledge inside me.
jokes.setup: Marge expressing enthusiasm for Professor August's teaching
jokes.punchline: Innocent statement with unintentional sexual undertones
jokes.explanation: Standard double entendre. Works for Marge's innocence but not particularly original or clever.

jokes.text: Good Goddess! The Patriots deep in Redskin territory. This isn't entertainment; it's genocide!
jokes.setup: Professor trying to relate to sports to seem normal
jokes.punchline: Turning football commentary into social justice critique
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing professor's inability to escape his academic worldview. Very quotable.

jokes.text: If I were to approach you for a kiss, would you construe that as harassment or give your willing consent?
jokes.setup: Professor trying to be romantic with Marge
jokes.punchline: Overly academic approach to requesting a kiss
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing how professor's academic mindset ruins romance. Painfully realistic.

jokes.text: Raisin bread with applesauce / Tony Danza, he's the boss / Brain freeze!
jokes.setup: Weird Al doing a parody of Homer's grunge song
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical lyrics that completely miss the original's tone
jokes.explanation: Good Weird Al parody that captures his style of making dark songs absurdly cheerful. Meta-humor works well.

jokes.text: He who is tired of Weird Al is tired of life.
jokes.setup: Homer expressing depression despite Weird Al parody success
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's philosophical defense of Weird Al
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy line - pretentious but oddly profound. Great parody of Samuel Johnson quote.

jokes.text: Everything penis-shaped is bad.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining lighthouse as phallocentric technocracy
jokes.punchline: His reductive feminist interpretation of all cylindrical objects
jokes.explanation: Economical satire of extreme academic feminism. Character-perfect and quotably absurd.

jokes.text: Slavery is alive and well in Springfield.
jokes.setup: Seeing a wedding
jokes.punchline: Professor's dramatic interpretation of marriage ceremony
jokes.explanation: Consistent character - professor finding oppression in every normal social institution.

jokes.text: Oh, it's statements like that make people say women are stupid.
jokes.setup: Marge saying she'd like to get married someday
jokes.punchline: Professor's condescending response disguised as feminism
jokes.explanation: Excellent revelation of professor's true misogyny. Shows how pseudo-feminism can be more sexist than overt sexism.

jokes.text: Or should I say, Associate Professor Jerk!
jokes.setup: Marge calling Professor August 'Professor Jerk'
jokes.punchline: Adding academic rank insult to make it more cutting
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that hits professor where it hurts most - his academic status. Great character moment for Marge.

jokes.text: I only got denied tenure because my department head is an idiot!
jokes.setup: Marge's insult about his associate professor rank
jokes.punchline: Professor's defensive revelation about his career failures
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing professor's insecurity and explaining his bitterness. Character development through comedy.

jokes.text: I only got denied tenure because my department head is an idiot!
jokes.setup: Marge's insult about his academic rank
jokes.punchline: His defensive explanation revealing professional failure
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation - professor's arrogance masks professional insecurity and failure.

jokes.text: You're about to get your PhD... Pound Head Down.
jokes.setup: Department head appearing to threaten Professor August
jokes.punchline: PhD redefined as physical violence threat
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that subverts academic credentials into violence threat. Well-structured pun with good setup.

jokes.text: Mar-ge-rine!
jokes.setup: Homer's emotional song about Marge
jokes.punchline: The pun on her name with margarine
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer romantic gesture - heartfelt but undermined by terrible pun that somehow makes it more endearing.

jokes.text: This is Kurt Loder reporting from... the '90s.
jokes.setup: MTV news report about Homer's band
jokes.punchline: The vague, period-appropriate sign-off
jokes.explanation: Good period joke - Kurt Loder was synonymous with MTV news, and the vague decade reference is perfect.

jokes.text: Unfortunately, as I later learned, that wasn't heroin. It was insulin. Homer really did need those injections.
jokes.setup: Marge 'saving' Homer from drug addiction
jokes.punchline: Revealing she was preventing necessary medical treatment
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy misdirection. Perfect structure with genuine shock value and character-driven humor.

jokes.text: I had become diabetic from drinking too many Frappuccinos.
jokes.setup: Homer's insulin dependence revealed
jokes.punchline: 90s coffee culture causing diabetes
jokes.explanation: Good period-specific humor about 90s coffee trends. Character-appropriate excess leading to consequences.

jokes.text: At least we know there'll never be a president worse than Bill Clinton. Imagine, lying in a deposition in a civil lawsuit. That's the worst sin a president could commit. There'll never be a worse president. Never.
jokes.setup: Characters discussing future optimism in the '90s
jokes.punchline: Their confident prediction about presidential standards that audience knows was wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect historical irony - characters' innocent '90s optimism about political standards that proved hilariously naive.

jokes.text: At least we know there'll never be a president worse than Bill Clinton. Imagine, lying in a deposition in a civil lawsuit. That's the worst sin a president could commit. There'll never be a worse president. Never.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge discussing future concerns as 90s end
jokes.punchline: Their confident prediction about presidential standards
jokes.explanation: Brilliant hindsight humor that gains impact over time. Excellent dramatic irony for post-2016 audiences.

jokes.text: I mean, unless he had been a little nicer at the beach.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming professor couldn't keep him and Marge apart
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that better treatment might have worked
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's honesty about his relationship insecurities. Deadpan delivery works well.

jokes.text: Homie is where my heart is.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining what professor taught her
jokes.punchline: Home/Homer pun expressing her true feelings
jokes.explanation: Sweet wordplay that perfectly captures Marge's sentiment. Economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Townies.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge's happy reunion and dismissal of academic pretension
jokes.punchline: Professor's bitter final word showing his unchanged elitism
jokes.explanation: Perfect bitter ending for professor character. Economical callback that shows he learned nothing.
